page: Microsoft Azure FX64-32ms_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the FXmsv2 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 16 physical cores and 32 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) with hyper-threading enabled. It features a substantial memory allocation of 1344.0 GB, yielding 42.0 GB of RAM per physical core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is premium storage capable, and it does not include any graphics processing units (GPUs).

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core and memory performance. The instance achieves top-tier status in single-threaded gzip compression (34.84 GB/sec) and Geekbench single-core testing (2340.0). Large-block memory bandwidth is also strong, with read-only speeds reaching 239.18 GB/sec and write speeds at 136.4 GB/sec. Multi-threaded operations, such as bzip3 decompression, perform in the top 25% at 183.85 GB/sec. However, certain workloads show limitations; while small and medium model LLM prompt processing is strong, text generation using a large 70B model falls into the bottom 25% at 2.64 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX64-32ms_v2 offers a high density of memory relative to its compute cores, making it a specialized option for memory-bound workloads rather than general-purpose computing. The lack of bundled local storage means storage costs must be factored in separately via network-attached premium storage. The high clock-speed Intel Xeon processor delivers strong single-core performance, but the absence of hardware accelerators limits its efficiency for heavy parallel AI training or large-scale LLM text generation.

This server configuration is suited for memory-intensive applications, large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, and single-threaded data processing tasks. It is also applicable for medium-scale language model prompt processing and high-throughput static web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure FX64-32ms_v2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 16 physical cores, 32 vCPUs, and a substantial 1344.0 GB of RAM, providing 42.0 GB of memory per core. The instance lacks bundled local storage and GPUs, relying instead on network-attached premium storage. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2340.0 and single-threaded gzip compression of 34.84 GB/sec. Large-block memory bandwidth is strong, reaching 239.18 GB/sec for read operations. However, text generation for large 70B LLM models is weak at 2.64 tokens/sec. This server is designed for memory-intensive workloads, in-memory databases, and high-throughput data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX64-32ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, 1344 GB of RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor. It delivers strong single-core performance but lacks bundled local storage and GPU accelerators.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX64-32ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 32 vCPUs, 1344 GB of RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, lacking local storage.
tagline: A memory-intensive server featuring a high-frequency Intel Xeon processor and massive RAM capacity for demanding database and single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 32 vCPUs and 4.2 GHz clock speed
1344.0 GB of RAM providing high memory-to-core density
Top-tier single-core performance in compression and text processing
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware accelerators
Suited for in-memory databases and large-scale data processing
